perItemFindings.label: def:global-feasible-fiber-decision
perItemFindings.one_line: The core includes real-QE/CAD decision of the feasibility formula, but the plan deliberately defines only an existential Prop and supplies neither a CAD gate nor a conditional interface for the absent decision engine.

perItemFindings.label: def:direction-selector
perItemFindings.one_line: Its promised globally finite semialgebraic selector depends on the same absent QE/CAD engine; an Option-valued Prop definition without evaluability does not realize that construction.

perItemFindings.label: def:real-atlas-handle
perItemFindings.one_line: The plan retains only certificate/set fragments although this core handle explicitly runs CAD; it silently omits the research-scale real-closed-field decision substrate rather than gating it.

perItemFindings.label: thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery
perItemFindings.one_line: The notes call this a deferred >=3-file apolarity/catalecticant plus Zariski-locus project, yet defer_tier is false and no named external-theory conditional is planned.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:generic-separation
perItemFindings.one_line: This node is likewise declared DEFER because it requires the absent I-1/I-2 apparatus, but is marked build-inline rather than honestly routed as a sized external dependency.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:minimal-real-information-order
perItemFindings.one_line: Its claimed proof needs both absent apolarity infrastructure and the truncated Hamburger moment problem (I-4), while defer_tier is false.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:generic-exceptional-locus
perItemFindings.one_line: I-1/I-2 are acknowledged as absent research-scale algebraic-geometry infrastructure, but the deferred theorem is not converted to an explicit conditional/gate.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:real-exceptional-atlas
perItemFindings.one_line: The plan correctly identifies CAD/QE and the full truncated moment problem as absent; Causalean's MomentSliceSupport is only a two-moment support result, not a total fit, so these must be named external conditional substrate rather than deferred theorem debt.

verbatim_critique: The Causalean/Mathlib reuse decisions themselves are sound: no cited declaration is being used as a mismatched theorem, and the PO/SCM bypass is appropriate for this binary-form LvLiNGAM world. Re-plan the CAD-facing definitions and all I-1/I-2/I-4-dependent cruxes: classify the missing named theories as research-scale external, gate/thread only the irreducible external facts (without bundling local reductions), and mark the presently declared deferred cruxes honestly. Do not present Prop-only feasibility or a classical partial definition as the core's finite semialgebraic decision procedure.
